DISCOVERY 301, NOTICE WAS NOT GIVEN TO THE OTHER PARTY AFTER THE TENTATIVE RULING CAME OUT. THE MATTER WAS NOT ALLOWED TO BE ARGUED. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT JMDH REAL ESTATE OF SAN FRANCISCO II LLC'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS, DISMISSING ACTION OF PLAINTIFF, AND FOR REQUEST FOR ATTORNEYS' FEES IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,350.00 - TERMINANTING SANCTIONS ARE DENIED IN LIGHT OF THE LATE RESPONSES, BUT PLAINTIFF MUST PAY AN ADDITIONAL MONETARY SANCTION OF $675.00. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, NOT REPORTED

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT JMDH REAL ESTATE OF SAN FRANCISCO II LLC'S MOTION FOR STAY PENDING APPEAL IS GRANTED. THE COURT HAS INHERENT AUTHORITY UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 128(A)(3) AND (8) TO CONTROL THE CONDUCT OF THE PARTIES AND THE PROCEEDINGS BEFORE IT. THIS MOTION IS NOT A MOTION FOR RECONSIDERATION UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 1008 AND WAS PROPERLY SET IN THIS DEPARTMENT. STAYING THIS ACTION PENDING A FINAL DECISION BY THE APPELLATE DIVISION IN CASE NUMBER 634591 PROMOTES A JUST AND EFFICIENT RESOLUTION. ORDER SIGNED. (302/HEK) JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7077

Off Calendar Dec-12-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT JMDH REAL ESTATE OF SAN FRANCISCO II LLC'S MOTION FOR STAY PENDING APPEAL.
Appearances: Gregory M. Haynes (546.0777) for plaintiff and responding party; Daniel J. Schacht of Donahue Gallagher Woods (381.4161) for defendant and moving party; John S. Moot of Schwartz Semerdjian Ballard & Cauley (619.236.8821) appearing via Court Call for defendant and moving party.
Argued and the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: granted. The court has inherent authority under Code of Civil Procedure 128(a)(3) and (8) to control the conduct of the parties and the proceedings before it. This motion is not a motion for reconsideration under Code of Civil Procedure 1008 and was properly set in this department. Staying this action pending a final decision by the Appellate Division in case number 634591 promotes a just and efficient resolution. Order signed. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077
